mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during an economic expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during late-cycle phases yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate earnings and prompt anticipated monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or the monthly correlation exceeds +0.15 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annually validates corporate revenue forecasts and reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive value over a flat baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms sustainable labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample hit rate below 51% across rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during a monetary tightening cycle signal demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, triggering rapid negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.5%.
